mcqs.question: What is the length of an IPv6 address?

options.text: 32 bits
64 bits
128 bits
256 bits

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Flow Label field in the IPv6 header?

options.text: To specify the priority of the packet
To identify a flow of packets
To specify the route that the packet should take
To fragment large packets into smaller ones

mcqs.explanation: The Flow Label field is used to identify a flow of packets.

mcqs.question: What is the maximum length of the payload in an IPv6 datagram?

options.text: 65535 bytes
131072 bytes
262144 bytes
524288 bytes

mcqs.explanation: The maximum length of the payload in an IPv6 datagram is 65535 bytes.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the Hop-by-Hop Options Header in IPv6?

options.text: To specify options that need to be processed by every device along the path
To specify options that need to be processed by the destination device
To specify the route that the packet should take
To fragment large packets into smaller ones

mcqs.explanation: The Hop-by-Hop Options Header is used to specify options that need to be processed by every device along the path.

mcqs.question: What is the main difference between IPv4 and IPv6 datagrams?

options.text: IPv6 datagrams are larger than IPv4 datagrams
IPv6 datagrams have a longer header than IPv4 datagrams
IPv6 datagrams use a different address format than IPv4 datagrams
All of the above

mcqs.explanation: IPv6 datagrams are larger than IPv4 datagrams, have a longer header, and use a different address format.
